project_name: L39 — Cats vs Dogs Classification using PyTorch
notes: All code files MUST be under 150 lines — split if exceeded
Every function needs type hints and docstrings
Comments must be understandable by a 12-year-old
NumPy vectorization — NO basic Python loops for array operations
Local training: 2-hour HARD LIMIT per model
Local (CPU): ~3,000 images, 10 epochs default. Colab (GPU): ~25,000 images, 15 epochs default
Before push: multi-agent QA on Colab Notebook AND README
README must include Local vs Colab comparison for all 10 models

tasks.name: Create enhanced PRD.md
acceptance_criteria: 10 architectures fully specified with layer tables
Local vs Colab comparison requirements defined

tasks.name: Get student approval on PRD and tasks.json
Create full directory structure
acceptance_criteria: .gitignore covers secrets, cache, venv, large files, .pth models

tasks.name: Setup UV virtual environment and requirements.txt
acceptance_criteria: requirements.txt has exact versions for: torch, torchvision, numpy, matplotlib, seaborn, scikit-learn, Pillow, tqdm, pandas, python-dotenv, pyyaml

tasks.name: Create configuration module
tasks.description: Central configuration with all hyperparameters, paths, constants, and named magic numbers

contents: Color scheme constants for consistent graphs

acceptance_criteria: No magic numbers anywhere in the codebase — all in config

tasks.name: Create utility modules
acceptance_criteria: All paths are relative using pathlib
set_all_seeds sets torch, numpy, random seeds for reproducibility
Ring buffer logger limits log files per config

tasks.name: Implement dataset download and loading

contents: download_dataset() — downloads cats_and_dogs_filtered via tf.keras.utils.get_file() OR direct URL
__init__: loads file paths and labels from directory structure
__getitem__: loads image, applies transforms, returns (image_tensor, label)
Handles both Colab and Local paths
Filters out corrupted images with try/except

acceptance_criteria: Dataset downloads successfully in both Colab and Local
Handles corrupted images gracefully (skip with warning)
Works with both grayscale and RGB transforms

contents: Augmentation applied ONLY to training, never validation

tasks.name: Implement DataLoader creation

contents: shuffle=True for training, False for validation

acceptance_criteria: Returns both loaders ready for training

tasks.name: Create dataset sample visualization

contents: plot_sample_images(dataloader, num_images=16) — grid of sample cats and dogs
plot_class_distribution(dataset) — bar chart showing cat/dog counts
plot_image_with_prediction(image, true_label, predicted_label) — single image with labels
plot_predictions_grid(images, true_labels, pred_labels, num_images=12) — grid with correct/incorrect coloring
All functions save to results/graphs/ AND display inline (for Colab)

acceptance_criteria: Sample grid shows 8 cats + 8 dogs with labels
Predictions grid highlights correct (green) and incorrect (red)

tasks.name: Model 1 — Baseline CNN (Keras→PyTorch conversion)

contents: Exact conversion of instructor's Keras example
Docstring explaining layer-by-layer what happens to the image

acceptance_criteria: Architecture exactly matches Keras example from PRD

tasks.name: Model 2 — Shallow CNN (2 Conv layers)
acceptance_criteria: Works with both RGB and Grayscale input

tasks.name: Model 3 — Deep CNN (6 Conv layers)
acceptance_criteria: Spatial dimensions don't go negative (pooling placement correct)

tasks.name: Model 4 — Wide CNN
acceptance_criteria: Fewer layers but more filters per layer than baseline

tasks.name: Model 5 — Small FC Variant

contents: 3 conv layers (32→64→128) + small FC head (128 neurons)

acceptance_criteria: FC layer has only 128 neurons (much smaller than baseline's 512)

tasks.name: Model 6 — CNN + Dropout

contents: Dropout(0.5) after Flatten, Dropout(0.3) after FC1

acceptance_criteria: Dropout layers present at correct positions

contents: BatchNorm2d after every Conv2d, before ReLU
Tests training stability and speed improvement
~3.46M parameters (slightly more due to BN params)

tasks.name: Model 8 — LeNet-Style CNN
acceptance_criteria: FC layer sizes match LeNet-5 tradition (120, 84)

tasks.name: Model 9 — Transfer Learning (ResNet18)

contents: Replace final FC: Linear(512→256) + ReLU + Dropout(0.3) + Linear(256→2)
Only custom head is trainable (~130K trainable params)
Input MUST be RGB (3 channels) — document this clearly
Handle input size: ResNet expects 224x224 but we use 150x150 — add adaptive pooling or resize

acceptance_criteria: Conv layers frozen (not updated during training)
Works with 150×150 input (may need adaptive pooling)

tasks.name: Model 10 — Lightweight Efficient Model
acceptance_criteria: Uses Global Average Pooling (eliminates large FC layers)

tasks.name: Create model registry in __init__.py

contents: MODEL_REGISTRY dict mapping model_id → (class, name, description, group)
get_model_info(model_id) → dict with name, description, group

acceptance_criteria: All 10 models accessible via get_model(1) through get_model(10)
Models support both input_channels=1 (grayscale) and input_channels=3 (RGB)

tasks.name: Implement training loop with time limit

contents: train_one_epoch() — single epoch training with batch progress
- Per-epoch train/val accuracy and loss tracking
- History dict returned: {train_acc, val_acc, train_loss, val_loss} per epoch

acceptance_criteria: Training loop works on both GPU and CPU
Time limit enforced: stops training if estimated total > 2 hours (local only)
Early stopping works: stops if val_loss doesn't improve for patience epochs
History dict contains all metrics per epoch
Progress bars show epoch, batch, loss, accuracy, time
Best model saved automatically based on best val_accuracy

tasks.name: Implement evaluation and metrics

contents: get_misclassified_samples(model, dataloader, device, num=6) → wrong predictions with images
Uses sklearn.metrics for confusion_matrix and classification_report
Returns comprehensive metrics dict with accuracy, precision, recall, f1, confusion_matrix

acceptance_criteria: Confusion matrix correct for binary classification (2×2)
Precision, recall, F1 computed per class (cat, dog)
Misclassified samples returned with images for visualization

tasks.name: Implement main.py entry point for local execution

contents: 4. Generate comparison visualizations (if all models run)

acceptance_criteria: python main.py runs all 10 models with defaults
Graceful error handling if one model fails (continue to next)
Total results saved to results/ directory

tasks.name: Training history plots (per model)

contents: plot_accuracy_curves(history, model_name) — train + val accuracy vs epoch
plot_loss_curves(history, model_name) — train + val loss vs epoch
All plots: title, labels, legend, grid, annotations (best epoch marked)
Save to results/graphs/ as PNG 300 DPI
Return figure for inline Colab display

acceptance_criteria: Accuracy plot shows train AND validation on same axes
Loss plot shows train AND validation on same axes
Best epoch annotated with marker and text
Overfitting visible when train >> val (gap highlighted)

tasks.name: Confusion matrix plots

contents: Annotations: count AND percentage in each cell

acceptance_criteria: Clear color distinction between high and low values

tasks.name: Cross-model comparison charts

contents: plot_accuracy_comparison(all_results) — bar chart all 10 models accuracy
plot_loss_comparison(all_results) — bar chart all 10 models final loss
plot_group_comparison(all_results) — grouped bar chart by research group
Consistent color scheme across all charts
Bars sorted by accuracy (best to worst)
Annotations with exact values on each bar

acceptance_criteria: All 10 models compared in each chart
Bars annotated with exact numerical values
Color-coded by research group (A=blue, B=green, C=orange, D=red)
Local vs Colab charts show side-by-side for same models
Scatter plot shows that bigger isn't always better

tasks.name: Update sample_plots.py with predictions grid
tasks.description: Add prediction visualization functions to the existing sample_plots.py from task 3.4
acceptance_criteria: Each image shows: true label, predicted label, confidence %

tasks.name: Create Colab notebook — Sections 1-4 (Setup, Data, Models, Training infra)
tasks.description: Convert all local source code into a self-contained Colab notebook. Sections 1-4 cover setup, data loading, model definitions, and training infrastructure.
acceptance_criteria: Section 1: Installs, imports, GPU detection, seeds
Section 2: Dataset download, visualization of samples, DataLoaders
Section 3: All 10 model classes defined with summaries
Markdown cells explain each section for educational value
Every code cell has a markdown cell above explaining what it does

tasks.name: Create Colab notebook — Section 5-6 (Training all models + Individual results)
tasks.description: Training loop for all 10 models with per-model result sections.
acceptance_criteria: Each model trains with progress bars
Per-model output: accuracy/loss curves, confusion matrix, sample predictions
Results stored in a dict for later comparison
Clear print of metrics after each model completes

tasks.name: Create Colab notebook — Section 7-8 (Comparison + Results Collection for README)
tasks.description: Cross-model comparison and the comprehensive Results Collection section designed for README integration. This is the MOST IMPORTANT section — it must be detailed enough to copy to Claude Code.
acceptance_criteria: Section 7: All comparison charts (accuracy, loss, params, time, scatter)
- Per-model results with all metrics and graphs
- Top-3 and Bottom-3 ranking with explanations
- Key findings (depth vs width, regularization, transfer learning, size vs accuracy)
- All text, tables, and graph references ready for README copy-paste
Section 8 output is clearly marked: '📋 COPY EVERYTHING BELOW FOR README'

tasks.name: QA Agent 1: Full execution test (Restart Runtime and Run All)
tasks.description: Run the entire notebook from scratch with 'Restart runtime and run all'. Must complete without ANY errors.
acceptance_criteria: Zero errors from first cell to last

tasks.name: QA Agent 2: Code quality and correctness review
tasks.description: Review all code for correctness, efficiency, and adherence to standards.
acceptance_criteria: No hardcoded values — all from config
Type hints present on all functions
NumPy vectorization (no Python loops for arrays)
Memory efficient (del unused tensors, torch.no_grad() in eval)

tasks.name: QA Agent 3: Educational content review
tasks.description: Review all markdown cells for educational quality — 12-year-old test.
acceptance_criteria: Every section has a clear educational intro
Comparison section has meaningful insights, not just numbers

tasks.name: QA Agent 4: Results accuracy and consistency review
tasks.description: Verify all results numbers are consistent across sections.
acceptance_criteria: Accuracy numbers in text match graph values
Parameter counts in summary match model definitions
Training times are realistic and consistent
Confusion matrix totals match dataset size

tasks.name: Run all 10 models locally with UV venv
tasks.description: Execute python main.py to train all 10 models locally. Each model has 2-hour time limit. Record all results.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 models run (some may hit time limit — that's OK)
Training log records epochs completed, time, accuracy per model
Models that hit time limit clearly marked in results

tasks.name: Generate Local vs Colab comparison data
tasks.description: Combine local results with Colab results to create comparison tables and charts.
acceptance_criteria: CSV with columns: model_name, local_accuracy, colab_accuracy, local_time, colab_time, local_epochs, colab_epochs
Comparison graphs generated (accuracy side-by-side, time side-by-side)
Notes on which models hit local time limit

tasks.name: Generate Local vs Colab comparison charts
tasks.description: Create visual comparison charts for Local vs Colab results.
acceptance_criteria: Side-by-side bar chart: accuracy per model (Local vs Colab)
Side-by-side bar chart: training time per model (Local vs Colab)
Table showing epoch counts (Local may have fewer due to time limit)

tasks.name: Write README Sections 1-7 (Intro, Education, Setup)
tasks.description: Title, Abstract, Applications, Features, Educational sections (What is CNN, How computer sees images, Layer explanations, Dropout, BatchNorm, Transfer Learning, PyTorch vs Keras), Environment setup, Installation instructions.
acceptance_criteria: Step-by-step installation for BOTH Local (UV) and Colab

tasks.name: Write README Sections 8-10 (Models, Results, Comparison)
tasks.description: 10 model descriptions, per-model results with graphs, cross-model comparison, Local vs Colab comparison, code files table.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 models described with architecture tables
Every result graph embedded as image
Cross-model comparison table with all 10 models
Code files summary table with line counts
All images load correctly from results/graphs/

tasks.name: Write README Sections 11-end (Learnings, Troubleshooting, Git)
tasks.description: What I learned, Troubleshooting, Author, License, GitHub setup instructions.
acceptance_criteria: Troubleshooting covers common issues (module not found, GPU not available, etc.)
All sections from PRD Section 10 are covered

tasks.name: QA Agent 1: 12-Year-Old Readability Agent
tasks.description: Review entire README for language simplicity. Every technical term must have a simple explanation. No assumed knowledge. Short sentences.
acceptance_criteria: Every technical term has an analogy
No sentences longer than 25 words without a break

tasks.name: QA Agent 2: Technical Accuracy Agent
tasks.description: Verify all numbers, architecture details, and commands are correct.
acceptance_criteria: All accuracy % match actual run results

tasks.name: QA Agent 3: Completeness Agent
tasks.description: Verify every required section from PRD Section 10 is present.

tasks.name: QA Agent 4: Professional Polish Agent
tasks.description: Check formatting, image rendering, links, tables, spelling.

tasks.name: QA Agent 5: Comparison & Analysis Agent
tasks.description: Review all comparison sections for depth and meaningful analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Local vs Colab comparison is comprehensive
Key findings are meaningful (not just restating numbers)

tasks.name: Fix all issues found by QA agents and re-review
tasks.description: Address every issue found by the 5 QA agents. Re-run checks to confirm fixes.

tasks.name: Pre-push checklist verification
tasks.description: Run through the complete pre-push checklist from PRD Section 19.3.
acceptance_criteria: No large files (>50MB) — .pth models in .gitignore if needed

tasks.name: Clear cache and fresh test
tasks.description: Clear all __pycache__, .pyc files. Test main.py --model 1 on fresh environment.
acceptance_criteria: main.py --model 1 runs successfully after cache clear

tasks.name: Git init, add, commit, push
tasks.description: Initialize git repo, add all files, commit, push to GitHub.
acceptance_criteria: git add . includes all required files
Verify on GitHub: README renders, images display, structure correct

tasks.name: Post-push GitHub verification
tasks.description: Open the GitHub repo in browser and verify everything looks correct.
acceptance_criteria: No secrets or large binary files committed

dependency_graph_summary.description: Simplified dependency chain showing critical path
critical_path: 1.3 (approval) → 2.1 (structure) → 2.3 (config) → 3.1 (dataset) → 4.1 (baseline model) → 4.11 (registry) → 5.1 (trainer) → 5.3 (main.py) → 7.1 (colab start) → 7.3 (colab results) → 8.1 (QA colab) → 9.1 (local run) → 10.1 (README start) → 11.1 (QA readme) → 12.3 (push)
parallel_work: Models 4.2–4.10 can be done in parallel after 4.1 is complete
Visualization tasks 6.1–6.4 can be done in parallel
QA agents 8.1–8.4 and 11.1–11.5 run in parallel
